completeness.evidence: All 6 required output files present (plan.md, timeline.md, budget.md, logistics.md, partner-brief.md, test-report.json). No [TBD]/[TODO]/XXX placeholders found in any file. Contact details in partner-brief.md Section 9 are now populated (jay@filamentree.com, LinkedIn confirmed). Section numbering in logistics.md was corrected (1→2→3→4→5→6→7→8). NPS claim in partner-brief.md correctly labeled as target benchmark with real parent quote added. R10 and R11 added to risk matrix. DEDUCTION AVOIDED: No mandatory -3 trigger. RESIDUAL ISSUE: plan.md Section 5.4 contains ₩57,965,000 — a figure derived from the OLD ₩160,000 flat variable cost, while budget.md was updated to ₩172,000 blended. The stale cross-document figure was not caught by test-report.json (which validated budget.md arithmetic in isolation, not cross-file consistency). This is not a placeholder but it IS an internal inconsistency that makes the plan factually disagree with the budget. Score capped at 8 (cannot reach 9 with a cross-file math error).

feasibility.evidence: Budget arithmetic within budget.md is internally consistent after the Section 2.1b addition. Blended variable cost (₩172,000) is correctly used in Section 5.1 and 5.2. Break-even at 4.7 sessions/month remains realistic. REMAINING GAP 1: Section 3.1 base assumption header explicitly states '₩160,000 variable cost' — the same section that was meant to frame the canonical revenue scenario analysis. A reader comparing Section 3.1 contribution margins (₩190,000 at 30/70 with ₩160K) against Section 5.2 projections (using ₩172K) will see inconsistent margins without explanation. The reconciliation exists in Section 2.1b but requires a reader to hold two numbers simultaneously. REMAINING GAP 2: The Summer 2027 pilot launch (W53: 2027-07-19) depends on a 6-month procurement outcome at the optimistic end of the 6-12 month range stated in the plan itself — procurement risk is described but not modeled in the financial projections (no contingency scenario for 12-month delays pushing launch to Q1 2028, which would mean 12+ months of no revenue against ₩27.8M setup cost recovery target).

differentiation.evidence: C07 is genuinely differentiated from base SPEC and from C01 (full 4-hour in-house workshop). Core innovations: 90-minute compressed popup with template-based game system, walk-in dual-channel booking, revenue-share model with park operator, explicit taster→full workshop funnel, Chosun Ilbo competitive urgency argument. Cross-case duplicate rate confirmed <25% in test-report.json. The curriculum compression analysis (Section 1.3) explicitly documents what was adapted and why — this is the kind of case-specific thinking that earns differentiation credit. MINOR NOTE: The risk matrix format (Likelihood/Impact/Mitigation columns, numbered R1-R11) is structurally generic, but this is a formatting choice, not a content duplication issue.

partner_value.evidence: Partner brief for THEME PARK is strong: educational content obligation framing, zero cost to park, ₩45-67.5M annual park revenue, Dong-A Ilbo co-branding, first-mover competitive urgency vs Chosun Ilbo. These are compelling and specific. Contact info now confirmed. NPS claim correctly labeled as target benchmark with verbatim parent quote added. PERSISTENT STRUCTURAL GAP (third iteration unresolved — escalating): SPEC.md Section 1 identifies 'Dong-A Ilbo' as THE primary partner. C07 generates only ₩12,526,000/year for Dong-A Ilbo (25% of ₩50,105,000 operating profit). In exchange, Dong-A must provide: brand name, PR support, and park negotiation leverage (per plan.md Section 4.4). No document in the C07 case files makes the internal business case TO Dong-A Ilbo — why should they invest their education brand's credibility in a theme park entertainment context? Compared to C01 (Dong-A Media Center, direct workshop operation), C07 gives Dong-A 1/4 the revenue share for a riskier, harder-to-control deployment channel. The partner-brief.md is entirely addressed to the THEME PARK. A Dong-A education executive reading the full case file has no document that answers 'why should Dong-A participate in this?'. This is the primary partner per SPEC and they have no dedicated value proposition. Score reduced from 7 to 6 due to escalation.

risk_coverage.evidence: 11 risks now identified (R1-R11) after R10 (학원법 regulatory) and R11 (AI-generated IP ownership) were added in the previous revision. All 11 include Likelihood, Impact, and Mitigation. R10 and R11 are genuine additions — not just format-filling. R10's mitigation (engage education law counsel, restructure fee as 'experience admission' rather than workshop tuition) is concrete and follows the KidZania fee-structuring model as a real precedent. R11's mitigation (explicit IP assignment clause in participant waiver with license back to participant) is actionable. MINOR WEAKNESS: R3 mitigation ('accept that conversion is secondary to brand exposure') and R8 ('start BD early') are still acknowledgments rather than risk controls. They describe the situation, not what we do when the risk materializes. At 11 risks total, however, coverage is substantive enough that this does not trigger a deduction.

actionability.evidence: Timeline spans W1 (2026-07-01) through W52 (2027-07-18) with exact date ranges, named owners, and explicit dependencies. Critical path diagram is accurate. Seasonal calendar gives monthly sessions/day breakdown. REMAINING GAP 1: W1-2 milestone ('Research: Map theme park education zone contacts') names no concrete deliverable. A team member executing this milestone has no success criterion — does it end when they send one email, or when they have a list of 10 named decision-makers? The equivalent milestone in a corporate BD context would specify: 'Deliverable: target contact list with minimum 3 named decision-makers per park (name, title, LinkedIn URL, estimated outreach timing).' REMAINING GAP 2 (ESCALATED from iteration 7 — not fixed): The 90-minute curriculum relies critically on 'Game Design Cards' (3-field pre-printed card) and 'Bad/Good Instruction card sets' as physical assets. These are referenced in plan.md Section 1.2, plan.md Section 1.4 template system, logistics.md Section 2.2, and logistics.md Section 3.1 turnover protocol. W45-46 in timeline.md ('90-min curriculum finalization') is the milestone for creating these. However, no file in the C07 case contains a content spec, field list, or design brief for these cards. A team executing W45-46 has no starting point. If the Game Design Card is poorly designed (wrong fields, wrong reading level, too complex for 5-minute use), the core 'Flash Planning' phase of the 90-minute session fails.

issues.location: Section 5.4 'Funnel Value Calculation', row '90-min session revenue (per park, 70% share at ₩50,000)'
issues.problem: plan.md Section 5.4 states '₩57,965,000/year (see budget.md)' as the annual session revenue figure. This figure is mathematically derived from the OLD ₩160,000 flat variable cost (655 sessions × ₩160K = ₩104.8M variable; ₩173.565M our 70% share - ₩104.8M - ₩10.8M fixed = ₩57,965,000). However, budget.md was updated in a previous revision to use ₩172,000 blended variable cost (Section 2.1b), producing an annual operating profit of ₩50,105,000 — a ₩7,860,000 (13.5%) discrepancy. plan.md was NOT updated when budget.md was revised. This stale figure also propagated into test-report.json's architectural_findings (#2: 'direct session revenue (₩57.9M/year)'), meaning the tester validated it without catching the cross-file inconsistency. The plan claims ₩57.965M where the budget now shows ₩50.105M — any reviewer who cross-references the two documents will find an unexplained ₩7.86M gap.
issues.fix_instruction: In plan.md Section 5.4 'Funnel Value Calculation', update the row '90-min session revenue (per park, 70% share at ₩50,000)' from '₩57,965,000/year (see budget.md)' to '₩50,105,000/year (see budget.md Section 5.2 — using ₩172,000 blended variable cost per session)'. Also update the 'Total channel value' calculation: ₩50,105,000 + ₩9,690,000 funnel revenue = ₩59,795,000 (not ₩67,655,000). Finally, update test-report.json architectural_findings item #2 to replace '₩57.9M/year' with '₩50.1M/year' to prevent the stale figure from re-appearing in downstream documents.

issues.location: Section 3.1 'Three Revenue Share Models', base assumption header
issues.problem: Section 3.1 header reads: 'Base assumption: 10 teams/session, ₩50,000/team = ₩500,000 gross/session, ₩160,000 variable cost'. This contradicts Section 2.1b (added in a previous revision), which established that the correct blended variable cost is ₩172,000/session. As a result, the contribution margins in Section 3.1 are overstated for all three models: Model A shows ₩190,000 margin (using ₩160K) vs the correct ₩178,000 (using ₩172K); Model B shows ₩140,000 vs correct ₩128,000; Model C shows ₩90,000 vs correct ₩78,000. A reader using Section 3.1 for negotiation planning (the primary purpose of this section) would enter a park meeting with contribution margins that are 7-15% too optimistic. The fix was applied in Section 5 projections but not retroactively in Section 3.
issues.fix_instruction: In budget.md Section 3.1, update the base assumption header to: 'Base assumption: 10 teams/session, ₩50,000/team = ₩500,000 gross/session, ₩172,000 blended variable cost (base ₩160K × 80% + peak ₩220K × 20% — see Section 2.1b)'. Recalculate the three model contribution margins: Model A: ₩350,000 - ₩172,000 = ₩178,000 (51%); Model B: ₩300,000 - ₩172,000 = ₩128,000 (43%); Model C: ₩250,000 - ₩172,000 = ₩78,000 (31%). Add a note: 'Off-peak sessions may realize ₩190,000 margin (base ₩160K cost); peak sessions ₩130,000 (₩220K cost). Annual blended using ₩172K shown in Section 5.'

issues.file: partner-brief.md (missing — no Dong-A Ilbo facing document in any file)
issues.location: All C07 case files — structural omission
issues.problem: ESCALATED from medium (iteration 7) — unresolved after 3 iterations. SPEC.md Section 1 identifies Dong-A Ilbo as THE primary partner ('파트너: 동아일보'). C07 is a deployment of the Academy through this partnership. Yet no document in the C07 case makes the internal business case TO Dong-A Ilbo for participating in C07 rather than, or in addition to, C01. The partner-brief.md is addressed exclusively to theme parks (Everland/Lotte World). Dong-A Ilbo receives ₩12,526,000/year (25% of ₩50,105,000 operating profit) from this channel. In exchange, they provide: brand name, PR support, and park negotiation leverage. A Dong-A education executive comparing C07 to C01 would observe: C07 gives Dong-A 1/4 the revenue share on a theme park entertainment context with potential brand dilution risk (plan.md R4 explicitly acknowledges this risk), while C01 offers full brand control in the Dong-A Media Center. Without an internal value case for Dong-A, this case is an incomplete pitch — it convinces the theme park but not the primary partner who must approve and support the deployment.
issues.fix_instruction: Add a new section to plan.md titled 'Section 9: Dong-A Ilbo Value Case' (or alternatively create a 1-page internal memo named dong-a-value-case.md). This section must answer: (1) Why C07 over more C01 sessions? (Strategic: theme park exposure reaches 1M+ visitors not reachable through Dong-A Media Center events — different audience entirely, not cannibalistic.) (2) What does Dong-A get that justifies their brand involvement? (a) ₩12.5M passive annual income with zero Dong-A operational burden, (b) ~78.8M/year in co-branded park impressions, (c) first-mover AI education positioning in mass entertainment venues before Chosun Ilbo moves, (d) pipeline of theme park families into Dong-A's education ecosystem.) (3) What is the brand risk ceiling? (Define: what quality failures would trigger Dong-A's right to suspend co-branding per contract?) This does not need to be a long document — 1 page covering these 3 questions is sufficient.

issues.location: plan.md Section 1.2 (Flash Planning, Context Training) and logistics.md Section 2.2 (Consumable Materials)
issues.problem: ESCALATED from actionability evidence (iteration 7 noted this was unresolved). The 90-minute curriculum depends on two physical assets that are referenced throughout the case but never defined: (1) 'Game Design Card' — plan.md Section 1.2 specifies it has '3 fields only: Game Name, Main Character, How to Win' and that it enables 5-minute Flash Planning. The success of the entire compressed Part 2 depends on this card being well-designed for the target age group (8-15 year olds with park excitement/distraction). (2) 'Bad/Good Instruction card sets' — referenced as a 'laminated, reusable' set used in 10-minute Context Training (plan.md Section 1.2 Row 0:10). The number of cards, their prompts, and the 'card flip game' mechanics are never specified. A staff member executing W45-46 ('90-min curriculum finalization') cannot begin without knowing what these materials should contain. These are not logistical overhead — they ARE the compressed curriculum.
issues.fix_instruction: In plan.md Section 1.4 (Template System), add a parallel subsection '1.5 Physical Asset Specifications': For the Game Design Card: (a) dimensions (A5 recommended — fits on small desk without interfering with laptop), (b) field layout (Game Name: blank line 3cm; Main Character: checkbox options [hero/animal/vehicle/robot] + custom blank; How to Win: checkbox options [collect items/survive longest/reach destination/defeat enemies] + custom blank; optional: 'One word describing your game's feeling' for emotional hook). For the Bad/Good Instruction Card set: (a) 5-card pairs minimum (one 'bad' instruction + one 'good' instruction version of same request), (b) example pair: BAD: 'Make it better' vs GOOD: 'Make the character jump 2x higher and add a coin sound effect'; (c) cards should be A6 size, color-coded (red for bad, green for good), laminated for reuse across sessions. Alternatively, tag these as 'Deliverables of W45-46' and require JY (Content Lead) to produce printable PDFs by W44 for budget and logistics approval.

issues.problem: Section 8.2 KPI: 'Annual revenue (our share, 60% model) | ₩36,000,000+'. This KPI is based on Model B (40/60 revenue split — park 40% / us 60%), but the entire business case, budget projections, and negotiation recommendation throughout C07 are based on Model A (30/70 split — park 30% / us 70%). Model B is described as the 'maximum fallback' in budget.md Section 3.1 and plan.md Section 4.2. Setting the primary steady-state KPI at the fallback model creates an internal contradiction: if management approves C07 based on Model A projections (₩50M operating profit) but the KPI dashboard measures against Model B (₩36M floor), there will be a false 'success signal' even if negotiations fall back to the suboptimal model. It also means the KPI target understates actual performance expectation by ₩14M/year.
issues.fix_instruction: In plan.md Section 8.2, update the Annual Revenue KPI to reflect Model A (recommended model): 'Annual revenue (our share, 30/70 recommended model) | ₩50,000,000+'. Add a note: 'Floor KPI under Model B (40/60 fallback): ₩36,000,000+. Negotiation strategy: target Model A; accept Model B only if park provides significant ancillary benefits (free storage, marketing push, app featured placement) per plan.md Section 4.2.' This aligns the KPI with the negotiation strategy and prevents false success reporting if Model B is accepted.

revision_prompt:
C07 (Theme Park Popup Workshop) has reached strong operational quality but has 5 specific issues to fix before it is ready for both partner distribution AND internal Dong-A Ilbo approval. Execute all of the following:

1. FILE: plan.md, SECTION: Section 5.4 'Funnel Value Calculation'.
   CHANGE: Row '90-min session revenue (per park, 70% share at ₩50,000)' — update from '₩57,965,000/year' to '₩50,105,000/year (see budget.md Section 5.2 — ₩172,000 blended variable cost)'. Recalculate 'Total channel value': ₩50,105,000 + ₩9,690,000 = ₩59,795,000. This stale figure derives from the old ₩160,000 flat variable cost that was corrected in budget.md but not propagated to plan.md. Also fix test-report.json architectural_findings #2 ('₩57.9M/year' → '₩50.1M/year').

2. FILE: budget.md, SECTION: Section 3.1 'Three Revenue Share Models' base assumption header.
   CHANGE: Replace '₩160,000 variable cost' in the base assumption header with '₩172,000 blended variable cost (see Section 2.1b)'. Recalculate contribution margins: Model A ₩350K - ₩172K = ₩178K (51%); Model B ₩300K - ₩172K = ₩128K (43%); Model C ₩250K - ₩172K = ₩78K (31%). This eliminates the internal inconsistency between Section 3.1 (old numbers) and Sections 2.1b and 5 (updated numbers).

3. FILE: plan.md, SECTION: add new Section 9 'Dong-A Ilbo Value Case' (after Section 8).
   CREATE: A 1-page internal section addressing why Dong-A Ilbo should participate in C07 rather than focusing exclusively on C01. Required content: (a) Strategic non-cannibalism argument — theme park reaches 1M+ visitors who never attend a Dong-A Media Center event; (b) Dong-A passive income: ₩12.5M/year with zero operational burden; (c) 78.8M/year in co-branded park impressions; (d) first-mover AI education positioning in mass entertainment venues before Chosun Ilbo moves; (e) brand risk ceiling definition — what operational failure thresholds trigger Dong-A's right to suspend co-branding. Per SPEC.md, Dong-A Ilbo IS the primary partner — they need a dedicated value case.

4. FILE: plan.md, SECTION: add Section 1.5 'Physical Asset Specifications' after Section 1.4 (Template System).
   CREATE: Content specifications for two critical curriculum assets currently referenced but undefined: (a) Game Design Card: A5 size, 3 fields with constrained-choice layout (Game Name: free text; Main Character: 4 checkbox options + custom; How to Win: 4 checkbox options + custom); (b) Bad/Good Instruction Card set: minimum 5 pairs, A6 size, color-coded (red/green), laminated for reuse, with example pairs listed. Tag both as deliverables of timeline W45-46 (JY Content Lead). Without these specs, the W45-46 'curriculum finalization' milestone cannot be executed.

5. FILE: plan.md, SECTION: Section 8.2 'Steady-State Annual KPIs'.
   CHANGE: Annual revenue KPI from '₩36,000,000+ (60% model)' to '₩50,000,000+ (30/70 recommended model)' with a floor note: 'Floor under Model B fallback: ₩36M+'. This aligns the KPI with the recommended negotiation model and prevents false success signaling if Model B is accepted without deliberate trade-off acknowledgment.
